expectations.text: Output shows all 3 stages: finder scan, adversary challenges, and referee ruling
expectations.evidence: Stage 1: Finder Report, Stage 2: Adversary Report, Stage 3: Referee Verdict — all present with clear separation

expectations.text: Finder identifies SQL injection via string interpolation in the query
expectations.evidence: F-001: SQL Injection via string interpolation, rated BLOCKER

expectations.text: Finder identifies MD5 as insecure for password hashing
expectations.evidence: F-002: MD5 used for password hashing, rated BLOCKER

expectations.text: Finder identifies the token generation as predictable/insecure
expectations.evidence: F-004: Auth token is md5(username) — deterministic and forgeable, rated BLOCKER

expectations.text: Issues have IDs (F-001, F-002, etc.) with severity ratings
expectations.evidence: 8 issues F-001 through F-008 with BLOCKER/WARNING/SUGGESTION severity ratings

expectations.text: Adversary issues KILL/SURVIVE/DISPUTED verdicts for each finding
expectations.evidence: 7 SURVIVE verdicts, 1 DISPUTED (F-005) — all 8 findings addressed

expectations.text: Referee produces a final issue list with definitive severities
expectations.evidence: Final issue list table with 4 BLOCKER, 1 WARNING, 3 SUGGESTION — includes severity adjustments

expectations.text: Final verdict is NEEDS CHANGES (not APPROVED) given the security issues
expectations.evidence: Verdict: NEEDS CHANGES with 4 blockers identified

expectations.text: Debate metrics include finder accuracy % and false positive rate %
expectations.evidence: Finder accuracy: 100%, False positive rate: 0%, Adversary accuracy: 100%
